name: Spaghetti and Shrimp Florentine
recipeIngredient: optional extra virgin olive oil (1 tbsp)
1/3 cup roasted red bell pepper strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the shrimp with cool water and pat dry with paper towels. Sprinkle with salt and pepper, then set aside.
2. Cook the pasta al dente according to package directions. Drain well, return to the pot, and toss with olive oil if desired. Cover to keep warm.
3. While the pasta is cooking, coat a large nonstick skillet with olive oil cooking spray and heat over medium-high heat. Add garlic and stir-fry for 30 seconds, or until the garlic just begins to turn color. Add shrimp and stir-fry for about 4 minutes, or until the shrimp turn opaque and any liquid released from the shrimp during cooking has evaporated. Add wine, lemon juice, and oregano to the skillet, and cook for about 2 minutes, or until the volume is reduced by half.
4. Reduce heat to medium, add spinach, and stir-fry for a minute or 2, or just until the spinach is wilted. Stir in the roasted red bell pepper strips.
5. Add pasta to the skillet. In a small bowl, mix chicken broth with cornstarch. Pour the mixture over the pasta and toss gently for about 1 minute, or until the sauce thickens slightly. If the mixture seems too dry, add a little more broth. Remove from heat and serve hot, topping each serving with a tablespoon of Parmesan.
TIP: To roast peppers, rinse, dry well, and cut in half lengthwise. Remove stems, membranes, and seeds. Coat a baking sheet with nonstick cooking spray and place the peppers, cut side down, on the sheet. Bake at 425°F for 25 minutes, or until the skins are blistered and charred. Transfer to a bowl, cover tightly with a lid or plastic wrap, and let steam in the bowl at least 15 minutes. When cool enough to handle, gently peel the charred skins off (they should slip off easily). Dice or slice peppers as desired.
